sections.subtitle: Team Form and League Standing
sections.text: Napoli, sitting atop the Serie A table with an impressive 77 points, are in scintillating form with a streak of 4 consecutive wins and no losses in their last 9 matches. They've also managed to keep 4 clean sheets in a row at home, showcasing their defensive solidity. Genoa, on the other hand, languish in 13th place with just 39 points and are struggling with 3 consecutive losses and no wins in their last 4 away games. This stark contrast in form and league position heavily favors Napoli as they host this encounter at the Stadio Diego Armando Maradona in Naples.

sections.subtitle: Historical Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 5 matchups, Napoli have dominated Genoa with 3 wins and 2 draws, maintaining an unbeaten streak of 5 games against this opponent. The most recent clash on December 21, 2024, saw Napoli secure a 2-1 victory away at Genoa, with Frank Anguissa earning the Player of the Match title with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Head-to-head stats also indicate a trend of high-scoring games, with over 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 8 meetings, and both teams scoring in 5 of the last 6 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Napoli, Romelu Lukaku will be a major threat upfront with 12 goals scored this season and an expected goals value of 11.96, closely matching his output. In midfield, Scott McTominay, who has been in exceptional form with 5 goals in his last 5 matches and an average Sofascore rating of 7.96, will look to control the game alongside Frank Anguissa, who has 6 goals and a consistent rating of 7.08. Not to be overlooked is Matteo Politano, a standout in past games against Genoa with an average Sofascore rating of 7.52, 2 goals, and 1 assist in those encounters. On the Genoa side, Aarón Martín stands out with 5 assists and a league rank of 2 for key passes (59), while Mattia Bani impresses defensively with a ground duels won percentage of 73.7, also ranking 2nd in the league.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Style
sections.text: Napoli boast the best defensive record in the league, conceding the least goals (25) and holding the top rank for clean sheets (17). Their attacking efficiency is notable with a big chances conversion rate of 45.2%, the highest in Serie A, and they create 73 big chances, ranking 9th. They are also strong in the air, ranking 3rd for aerial duels won at 53.6%. Genoa, meanwhile, tend to score late, with 23.3% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could pose a threat if Napoli’s defense tires. However, Genoa’s away form struggles are evident with no clean sheets in their last 3 away games.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Insights
sections.text: Napoli have been remarkably disciplined, receiving the fewest yellow cards in the league (43) at an average of just 1.23 per game, with 8 of their last 9 matches seeing under 4.5 cards. Genoa, contrastingly, have a tendency for more bookings, with over 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 7 away games. In terms of set-pieces, Napoli average 5.46 corners per game, ranking 4th in the league, while head-to-head data suggests under 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 7 meetings with Genoa.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Missing Players
sections.text: Napoli will miss key midfielder Stanislav Lobotka, who has an average rating of 7.02, alongside defenders Alessandro Buongiorno and Juan Jesus, potentially weakening their squad depth. Genoa are without midfielder Fabio Miretti, who has contributed 3 goals and 3 assists this season, and defender Alan Matturro, which may impact their ability to compete effectively in Naples.
